_comment: Personal-data connectors example (ADR 0019/0020, service-access spec). Microsoft Graph (Tier A, delegated OAuth) + Gmail (Tier A Graph-style or Tier B IMAP/SMTP). All owner: user — Tessera holds the PERSON'S OWN login; never revealed to an agent or another user. Result classes (metadata/preview/full-body) live in Tessera.Core.Results: search/list returns metadata + handles, full body by handle only, sends are draft->confirm->step-up. Separate consent per data class (calendar consent must NOT grant mail). Real tenant/app config + tokens live in the homelab overlay; egress stays gated.

tools.name: graph_calendar_view
tools.description: List calendar events in a date range (metadata: subject, organizer, start/end; body by handle only).

rotation.detail: Tessera owns Graph token refresh via offline_access (Mode U).

recipes.description: Microsoft Graph calendar (Calendars.ReadBasic) — read:calendar; owner: user. Separate consent from mail.

tools.name: graph_mail_search
tools.description: Search mail — returns metadata + opaque handles only (no body).

tools.name: graph_mail_read
tools.description: Read one message's full body by handle (from a prior search).

tools.name: graph_mail_send
tools.description: Send a drafted message — draft/preview, get an explicit yes, then confirm=true.

recipes.description: Microsoft Graph mail (Mail.ReadBasic / Mail.Read / Mail.Send) — metadata-first; send is draft->confirm->step-up; owner: user.

tools.name: gmail_search
tools.description: Search mail — returns metadata + opaque handles only (no body).

tools.name: gmail_read
tools.description: Read one message's full body by handle (from a prior search).

tools.name: gmail_send
tools.description: Send a drafted message — draft/preview, get an explicit yes, then confirm=true.

recipes.description: Gmail (gmail.readonly / gmail.send) — metadata-first; send is draft->confirm->step-up; owner: user.
